methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Nano One Materials is a public-stage company (TSX: LNZA) in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised approximately C$100M to date. The valuation universe comprises 9 sector peers: 1 public company and 8 private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

The peer-median method was not applicable to this analysis. A search for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier peers within Climate & Energy yielded fewer than three datapoints in each filtered category, falling short of the minimum threshold needed for reliable triangulation. An all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of business models, capital intensity, and commercialization timelines across the Climate & Energy landscape.

Given the inapplicability of standard comparable methods, this valuation requires alternative approaches—such as discounted cash flow analysis anchored to the company's technology development milestones and addressable market, or transaction comps from recent funding rounds among the private peer set. The primary uncertainty remains the scarcity of directly comparable public benchmarks in this nascent, capital-intensive sector. Any valuation conclusion should be framed as directional pending deeper fundamental analysis or identification of additional peer data suitable for triangulation.
